title: quercus sessilis var. coronensis
intro: Quercus sessilis var. coronensis, also known as the Southern Live Oak, is a deciduous tree species native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Quercus sessilis var. coronensis include its potential to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease and certain cancers, as well as its ability to support digestive health and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Quercus sessilis var. coronensis can be found in various forms, including tea, tinctures, and capsules, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal dosages.
